task_completion.description: Navigate to the nodule location and adjust window/level to visualize lung tissue
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer view shows an axial slice with lung windowing (dark lungs with visible vessels/structures, not solid black or white) centered on a nodule.

task_completion.description: Create a Line markup using the Slicer toolbar or Markups module
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight line markup (usually yellow or colored) is visible in the slice viewer.

task_completion.description: Place the line endpoints at the edges of the nodule to measure diameter
task_completion.visual_evidence: The line markup spans the widest part of the rounded nodule opacity, with endpoints touching the nodule boundaries.

task_completion.description: Ensure the measurement value is visible in the Markups panel or view overlay
task_completion.visual_evidence: A numeric length value (in mm) is visible in the Markups module table or overlaid on the viewer next to the line.

task_completion.description: Verify the measured value falls within the plausible range for this dataset
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible measurement value is between 3.0 and 30.0 mm.

task_completion.description: Export the measurement to the specified JSON file using the Python console
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Python console is visible with code executing a JSON dump to '/home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/nodule_measurement.json'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Slicer GUI for measurement
integrity.visual_evidence: The Slicer application window is the primary focus, and the measurement line is visually present in the GUI, not just typed into a script.

integrity.description: Verify the exported value matches the visual measurement
integrity.visual_evidence: If the exported value is visible in the Python console, it matches the length shown in the Markups panel/overlay.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not look up the answer externally
integrity.visual_evidence: No web browser windows are open searching for 'LIDC-IDRI-0001 nodule size' or similar queries.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient ID: LIDC-IDRI-0001. Valid nodule diameter range: 3mm to 30mm. The nodule appears as a rounded light gray/white opacity against the dark lung background.
